members.summary: Configuration for every Vaswani-style Transformer variant.
members.extended:
The bare paper specifies a single 65 537-token shared BPE vocabulary for
WMT En-De and separate vocabularies for some tasks; we make this
configurable via `decoder_vocab_size` / `share_embeddings` so callers
can match whichever checkpoint they're porting.

members.name: TransformerForSeq2SeqLM

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(input_ids: Tensor, decoder_input_ids: Tensor, attention_mask: Tensor | None = None, decoder_attention_mask: Tensor | None = None, labels: Tensor | None = None)

parameters.name: input_ids
parameters.description: `(B, S)` source token ids.

parameters.name: decoder_input_ids
parameters.description: `(B, T)` target token ids (right-shifted).

parameters.name: attention_mask
parameters.description: Optional `(B, S)` HF-style padding mask.

parameters.name: decoder_attention_mask
parameters.description: Optional `(B, T)` HF-style padding mask.

returns.description: class:`Seq2SeqLMOutput` carrying decoder hidden states as

methods.name: generate
methods.signature: generate(input_ids: Tensor, max_length: int = 32, bos_token_id: int | None = None, eos_token_id: int | None = None, pad_token_id: int | None = None, attention_mask: Tensor | None = None, use_cache: bool = True, do_sample: bool = False, temperature: float = 1.0, top_k: int | None = None, top_p: float | None = None, repetition_penalty: float = 1.0)
methods.summary: Seq2seq decoding — greedy (default) or stochastic sampling.
methods.extended:
Encodes `input_ids` once, then unrolls the decoder one token at a
time (BOS-seeded) until `max_length` (or every batch row hits
`eos_token_id`).  Token selection routes through the shared sampling
primitives, so `do_sample` / `temperature` / `top_k` / `top_p` /
`repetition_penalty` behave exactly as for decoder-only models.

parameters.name: input_ids
parameters.description: `(B, S)` source token ids.

parameters.name: max_length
parameters.description: Cap on generated target length (incl. BOS).

parameters.name: bos_token_id
parameters.description:
Start-of-sequence id; defaults to
           `config.bos_token_id` then 0.

parameters.name: eos_token_id
parameters.description:
End-of-sequence id; defaults to
           `config.eos_token_id`.

parameters.name: pad_token_id
parameters.description:
Right-padding for finished rows; defaults to
           `config.pad_token_id` then 0.

parameters.name: attention_mask
parameters.description: Optional source padding mask.

parameters.name: use_cache
parameters.description:
Use an `EncoderDecoderCache` so each step only
           decodes the new token (default True).

parameters.name: do_sample
parameters.description:
Sample from the (filtered) distribution instead of
           greedy `argmax` (default False).

parameters.name: temperature
parameters.description: Logit temperature applied before sampling.

parameters.name: top_k
parameters.description: Keep only the `top_k` highest-probability tokens.

parameters.name: top_p
parameters.description:
Nucleus threshold — keep the smallest set whose
           cumulative probability reaches `top_p`.

parameters.name: repetition_penalty
parameters.description: Down-weight logits of already-generated tokens.

returns.description: `(B, T_out)` int Tensor of generated target tokens.

members.summary: Encoder-decoder Transformer with a tied seq2seq language-modeling head.
members.extended:
Wraps `TransformerModel` with a linear LM head projecting decoder
hidden states to the target vocabulary.  The head weight is tied to
`tgt_tok_emb.weight` when `config.tie_word_embeddings` is `True`,
matching the Vaswani 2017 §3.4 convention that halves the parameter cost
of the output softmax.  Standard interface for translation and
summarisation — pass `input_ids` (the source) and
`decoder_input_ids` (the right-shifted target); supply `labels` to
also compute the shift-loss for training.  Greedy inference is exposed
through `generate`.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Hyperparameters.  `config.tie_word_embeddings` (default True)
controls the LM-head weight tying.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.text.transformer import TransformerConfig, TransformerForSeq2SeqLM
>>> cfg = TransformerConfig(num_hidden_layers=2, num_decoder_layers=2,
...                         hidden_size=64, num_attention_heads=2,
...                         intermediate_size=128, vocab_size=1000)
>>> model = TransformerForSeq2SeqLM(cfg).eval()
>>> src = lucid.tensor([[1, 234, 567, 2]])
>>> tgt = lucid.tensor([[1, 100, 200]])
>>> out = model(src, decoder_input_ids=tgt)
>>> out.logits.shape   # (B=1, T_tgt=3, vocab=1000)
(1, 3, 1000)
notes:
Reference: Vaswani, Shazeer, Parmar, Uszkoreit, Jones, Gomez, Kaiser,
and Polosukhin, *"Attention Is All You Need"*, NeurIPS, 2017
([arXiv:1706.03762](https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.03762)).

Loss when `labels` is supplied is the per-token cross-entropy over
the right-shifted target sequence:



$$
\mathcal{L}_{\mathrm{S2S}}
    = -\frac{1}{BT} \sum_{b, t}
      \log p_\theta(y_{b, t} \mid x_b, y_{b, < t}),
$$

with positions labelled `-100` excluded.

attributes.name: lm_head
attributes.description:
Output projection of shape `(d_model, effective_decoder_vocab_size)`
mapping decoder hidden states to target-vocabulary logits.

members.name: TransformerForSequenceClassification

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(input_ids: Tensor, attention_mask: Tensor | None = None, labels: Tensor | None = None)

parameters.name: input_ids
parameters.description: `(B, S)` source token ids.

parameters.name: decoder_input_ids
parameters.description: `(B, T)` target token ids (right-shifted).

parameters.name: attention_mask
parameters.description: Optional `(B, S)` HF-style padding mask.

parameters.name: decoder_attention_mask
parameters.description: Optional `(B, T)` HF-style padding mask.

returns.description: class:`Seq2SeqLMOutput` carrying decoder hidden states as

members.summary: Encoder-only sentence classifier — pools the first source-side token.
members.extended:
Wraps `TransformerModel` and runs only the encoder half during
`forward`; the first source-side token's hidden state is
dropout-regularised and projected through a linear of output width
`config.num_labels`.  Pattern mirrors
`BERTForSequenceClassification` — the decoder half of the trunk
is unused so this head trains the same encoder weights efficiently for
GLUE-style fine-tunes on the Vaswani backbone.

parameters.name: config

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.text.transformer import (
...     TransformerConfig, TransformerForSequenceClassification,
... )
>>> cfg = TransformerConfig(num_labels=3, num_hidden_layers=2,
...                         hidden_size=64, num_attention_heads=2,
...                         intermediate_size=128, vocab_size=1000)
>>> model = TransformerForSequenceClassification(cfg).eval()
>>> input_ids = lucid.tensor([[1, 234, 567, 2]])
>>> out = model(input_ids)
>>> out.logits.shape   # (B=1, num_labels=3)
(1, 3)
notes:
Reference: Vaswani et al., *"Attention Is All You Need"*, NeurIPS, 2017
([arXiv:1706.03762](https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.03762)).

When `labels` is supplied, the loss is the standard cross-entropy:



$$
\mathcal{L} = -\frac{1}{B} \sum_{b=1}^{B}
    \log p_\theta(y_b \mid x_b).
$$

attributes.name: transformer
attributes.description: Underlying encoder-decoder trunk; only `encode` is invoked here.

attributes.name: dropout
attributes.description: Dropout layer applied to the pooled first-token embedding.

attributes.name: classifier
attributes.description:
Final linear of shape `(d_model, num_labels)` producing per-class
logits.

members.name: TransformerForTokenClassification

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(input_ids: Tensor, attention_mask: Tensor | None = None, labels: Tensor | None = None)

parameters.name: input_ids
parameters.description: `(B, S)` source token ids.

parameters.name: decoder_input_ids
parameters.description: `(B, T)` target token ids (right-shifted).

parameters.name: attention_mask
parameters.description: Optional `(B, S)` HF-style padding mask.

parameters.name: decoder_attention_mask
parameters.description: Optional `(B, T)` HF-style padding mask.

returns.description: class:`Seq2SeqLMOutput` carrying decoder hidden states as

members.summary: Encoder-only per-token classifier — NER / POS / chunking heads.
members.extended:
Wraps `TransformerModel` and runs only the encoder half during
`forward`; every source-side token's hidden state is
dropout-regularised and projected through a linear of output width
`config.num_labels`.  Uses `MaskedLMMixin` for the standard
masked `(B, T, V) → loss` reduction shared with BERT and RoFormer.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Hyperparameters.  `config.num_labels` sets the per-position output
dimension; `config.classifier_dropout` (falling back to
`hidden_dropout`) sets the dropout applied before the linear.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.text.transformer import (
...     TransformerConfig, TransformerForTokenClassification,
... )
>>> cfg = TransformerConfig(num_labels=9, num_hidden_layers=2,
...                         hidden_size=64, num_attention_heads=2,
...                         intermediate_size=128, vocab_size=1000)
>>> model = TransformerForTokenClassification(cfg).eval()
>>> input_ids = lucid.tensor([[1, 234, 567, 2]])
>>> out = model(input_ids)
>>> out.logits.shape   # (B=1, T=4, num_labels=9)
(1, 4, 9)
notes:
Reference: Vaswani et al., *"Attention Is All You Need"*, NeurIPS, 2017
([arXiv:1706.03762](https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.03762)).

Loss (when `labels` is provided) is the masked cross-entropy over
positions whose label is not `-100`:



$$
\mathcal{L} = -\frac{1}{|V|} \sum_{(b, t) \in V}
    \log p_\theta(y_{b, t} \mid x_b).
$$

attributes.name: transformer
attributes.description: Underlying encoder-decoder trunk; only `encode` is invoked here.

attributes.name: dropout
attributes.description: Dropout layer applied to the full sequence hidden states.

attributes.name: classifier
attributes.description:
Final linear of shape `(d_model, num_labels)` mapping each token's
hidden state to per-class logits.

members.name: TransformerModel

methods.name: get_output_embeddings
methods.summary: Target-side embedding — used to tie an LM head against.

methods.name: encode
methods.signature: encode(src_ids: Tensor, src_attention_mask: Tensor | None = None)
methods.summary: Run only the encoder; returns memory `(B, S, d_model)`.

methods.name: decode
methods.signature: decode(tgt_ids: Tensor, memory: Tensor, tgt_attention_mask: Tensor | None = None, memory_attention_mask: Tensor | None = None, past_key_value: EncoderDecoderCache | None = None, use_cache: bool = False)
methods.summary: Run only the decoder against precomputed encoder memory.
methods.extended:
When `use_cache` is set, `past_key_value` accumulates the decoder
self-attention keys/values (and caches the cross-attention projection
of `memory` once), so each step only needs the freshly appended
`tgt_ids` token(s).

parameters.name: tgt_ids
parameters.description: `(B, T)` target token ids to decode this step.

parameters.name: memory
parameters.description: `(B, S, d_model)` encoder output to cross-attend.

parameters.name: tgt_attention_mask
parameters.description: Optional `(B, T)` target padding mask.

parameters.name: memory_attention_mask
parameters.description: Optional `(B, S)` source padding mask.

parameters.name: past_key_value
parameters.description:
Optional `EncoderDecoderCache` accumulating
            decoder self-attention and the once-computed
            cross-attention key/value.

parameters.name: use_cache
parameters.description:
Read/write `past_key_value` so each step only
            decodes the freshly appended token(s).

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(input_ids: Tensor, decoder_input_ids: Tensor, attention_mask: Tensor | None = None, decoder_attention_mask: Tensor | None = None)

parameters.name: input_ids
parameters.description: `(B, S)` source token ids.

parameters.name: decoder_input_ids
parameters.description: `(B, T)` target token ids (right-shifted).

parameters.name: attention_mask
parameters.description: Optional `(B, S)` HF-style padding mask.

parameters.name: decoder_attention_mask
parameters.description: Optional `(B, T)` HF-style padding mask.

returns.description: class:`Seq2SeqLMOutput` carrying decoder hidden states as

members.extended:
Implements the canonical seq2seq architecture of Vaswani, Shazeer,
Parmar, Uszkoreit, Jones, Gomez, Kaiser, and Polosukhin, 2017.  A
stack of $N$ encoder layers (multi-head self-attention + FFN)
processes the source `input_ids`; a stack of $N$ decoder
layers (masked self-attention + cross-attention + FFN) consumes the
right-shifted `decoder_input_ids` and the encoder memory.  Token
embeddings are scaled by $\sqrt{d_{\text{model}}}$ and combined
with sinusoidal positional encodings before the trunk.

Forward inputs / outputs follow the `Seq2SeqLMOutput` contract.
For task-specific outputs use the `TransformerForXxx` wrappers; this
bare trunk is exposed so callers needing raw decoder hidden states
(e.g. for custom heads) can skip the default LM projection.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Hyperparameters controlling vocabularies, depth, width, head count,
max sequence length, and the `share_embeddings` /
`tie_word_embeddings` toggles.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.text.transformer import TransformerConfig, TransformerModel
>>> cfg = TransformerConfig(num_hidden_layers=2, num_decoder_layers=2,
...                         hidden_size=64, num_attention_heads=2,
...                         intermediate_size=128, vocab_size=1000)
>>> model = TransformerModel(cfg).eval()
>>> src = lucid.tensor([[1, 234, 567, 2]])
>>> tgt = lucid.tensor([[1, 100, 200]])
>>> out = model(src, decoder_input_ids=tgt)
>>> out.logits.shape   # decoder hidden (B=1, T_tgt=3, d_model=64)
(1, 3, 64)
>>> out.encoder_last_hidden_state.shape
(1, 4, 64)
notes:
Reference: Vaswani, Shazeer, Parmar, Uszkoreit, Jones, Gomez, Kaiser,
and Polosukhin, *"Attention Is All You Need"*, NeurIPS, 2017
([arXiv:1706.03762](https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.03762)).

Scaled dot-product attention:



$$
\mathrm{Attention}(Q, K, V) = \mathrm{softmax}\!\left(
    \frac{Q K^{\top}}{\sqrt{d_k}}
\right) V,
$$

with $d_k = d_{\text{model}} / h$.  The decoder applies an
additional lower-triangular causal mask on its self-attention so
position $t$ cannot attend to positions $> t$.

attributes.name: src_tok_emb
attributes.description: Source-side token embedding of shape `(vocab_size, d_model)`.

attributes.name: tgt_tok_emb
attributes.description:
Target-side token embedding.  Identical object as `src_tok_emb`
when `config.share_embeddings` is `True`.

attributes.name: positional_encoding
attributes.description:
Fixed sinusoidal positional encoding of size
`(max_position_embeddings, d_model)` (Vaswani 2017 §3.5).

members.name: transformer_base
members.extended:
Canonical seq2seq architecture from Vaswani, Shazeer, Parmar, Uszkoreit,
Jones, Gomez, Kaiser, and Polosukhin, 2017, Table 3 ("base" row):
$N=6$ encoder + $N=6$ decoder layers, $d_{\text{model}} = 512$, $h = 8$ heads, $d_{\text{ff}} = 2048$, dropout 0.1.
Roughly 65M parameters — the model that achieved SOTA on WMT 2014
En-De / En-Fr at a fraction of the training cost of prior recurrent
seq2seq systems.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description: Reserved for future weight registration; currently a no-op.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Optional `TransformerConfig` field overrides (e.g.
`vocab_size=...`, `decoder_vocab_size=...`,
`share_embeddings=True`) forwarded into the underlying config.

returns.description:
Encoder-decoder trunk configured with the "base" size and any
overrides.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.text.transformer import transformer_base
>>> model = transformer_base().eval()
>>> src = lucid.tensor([[1, 234, 567, 2]])
>>> tgt = lucid.tensor([[1, 100, 200]])
>>> out = model(src, decoder_input_ids=tgt)
>>> out.logits.shape   # (B=1, T_tgt=3, d_model=512)
(1, 3, 512)
notes:
Reference: Vaswani, Shazeer, Parmar, Uszkoreit, Jones, Gomez, Kaiser,
and Polosukhin, *"Attention Is All You Need"*, NeurIPS, 2017
([arXiv:1706.03762](https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.03762)), Table 3.

Multi-head attention:



$$
\mathrm{Attention}(Q, K, V) = \mathrm{softmax}\!\left(
    \frac{Q K^{\top}}{\sqrt{d_k}}
\right) V.
$$

members.name: transformer_base_cls
members.summary: Construct a Transformer "base" with a sequence-classification head.
members.extended:
Same trunk as `transformer_base` (N=6, d_model=512, h=8); only the
encoder half is used in `forward`.  The first source-side token's
encoder hidden state is dropout-regularised and projected through a
linear of output width `config.num_labels`.  Pattern mirrors
`BERTForSequenceClassification` and is appropriate for
GLUE-style fine-tunes on the encoder-only half of the Vaswani trunk.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description: Reserved for future weight registration; currently a no-op.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Optional `TransformerConfig` field overrides forwarded into
the underlying config.  Pass `num_labels=N` to set the number of
classes (default 2).

returns.description:
Encoder-only Transformer trunk wrapped with a pooled classifier
head.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.text.transformer import transformer_base_cls
>>> model = transformer_base_cls(num_labels=3).eval()
>>> input_ids = lucid.tensor([[1, 234, 567, 2]])
>>> out = model(input_ids)
>>> out.logits.shape   # (1, num_labels=3)
(1, 3)
notes: Reference: Vaswani et al., *"Attention Is All You Need"*, NeurIPS, 2017.

members.name: transformer_base_seq2seq
members.summary: Construct a Transformer "base" with a tied seq2seq-LM head.
members.extended:
Same trunk as `transformer_base` (N=6, d_model=512, h=8),
augmented with a linear LM head whose weight is tied to the target-side
embedding when `config.tie_word_embeddings` is `True`.  Provides a
`.generate(input_ids)` method for greedy seq2seq decoding (translation
/ summarisation inference).

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description: Reserved for future weight registration; currently a no-op.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Optional `TransformerConfig` field overrides forwarded into
the underlying config.

returns.description: Encoder-decoder trunk wrapped with the tied LM head.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.text.transformer import transformer_base_seq2seq
>>> model = transformer_base_seq2seq().eval()
>>> src = lucid.tensor([[1, 234, 567, 2]])
>>> tgt = lucid.tensor([[1, 100, 200]])
>>> out = model(src, decoder_input_ids=tgt)
>>> out.logits.shape   # (B=1, T_tgt=3, vocab=37000)
(1, 3, 37000)
notes:
Reference: Vaswani et al., *"Attention Is All You Need"*, NeurIPS, 2017.

Training loss when `labels` is supplied is the standard seq2seq
cross-entropy:



$$
\mathcal{L}_{\mathrm{S2S}}
    = -\frac{1}{BT} \sum_{b, t}
      \log p_\theta(y_{b, t} \mid x_b, y_{b, < t}),
$$

with positions labelled `-100` excluded.

members.name: transformer_base_token_cls
members.summary: Construct a Transformer "base" with a per-token classification head.
members.extended:
Same trunk as `transformer_base` (N=6, d_model=512, h=8); only the
encoder half is used in `forward`.  Every source-side token's hidden
state is dropout-regularised and projected through a linear of output
width `config.num_labels`, with the standard masked cross-entropy
inherited from `MaskedLMMixin`.  Use for sequence-labelling
tasks (NER, POS) when an encoder-only Vaswani trunk is preferred to
BERT.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description: Reserved for future weight registration; currently a no-op.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Optional `TransformerConfig` field overrides forwarded into
the underlying config.  Pass `num_labels=N` to set the tag set
size.

returns.description:
Encoder-only Transformer trunk wrapped with a per-token classifier
head.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.text.transformer import transformer_base_token_cls
>>> model = transformer_base_token_cls(num_labels=9).eval()
>>> input_ids = lucid.tensor([[1, 234, 567, 2]])
>>> out = model(input_ids)
>>> out.logits.shape   # (1, T=4, num_labels=9)
(1, 4, 9)
notes:
Reference: Vaswani et al., *"Attention Is All You Need"*, NeurIPS, 2017.

Loss (when `labels` is provided) is the masked cross-entropy over
positions whose label is not `-100`:



$$
\mathcal{L} = -\frac{1}{|V|} \sum_{(b, t) \in V}
    \log p_\theta(y_{b, t} \mid x_b).
$$

members.name: transformer_large
members.extended:
Larger of the two original variants from Vaswani et al., 2017, Table 3
("big" row): $N=6$ encoder + $N=6$ decoder layers,
$d_{\text{model}} = 1024$, $h = 16$ heads,
$d_{\text{ff}} = 4096$, dropout 0.3.  Roughly 213M parameters —
pushed BLEU on WMT 2014 En-De to 28.4 / En-Fr to 41.8 at release time.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description: Reserved for future weight registration; currently a no-op.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Optional `TransformerConfig` field overrides forwarded into
the underlying config.

returns.description:
Encoder-decoder trunk configured with the "big" size and any
overrides.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.text.transformer import transformer_large
>>> model = transformer_large().eval()
>>> src = lucid.tensor([[1, 234, 567, 2]])
>>> tgt = lucid.tensor([[1, 100, 200]])
>>> out = model(src, decoder_input_ids=tgt)
>>> out.logits.shape   # (B=1, T_tgt=3, d_model=1024)
(1, 3, 1024)
notes:
Reference: Vaswani, Shazeer, Parmar, Uszkoreit, Jones, Gomez, Kaiser,
and Polosukhin, *"Attention Is All You Need"*, NeurIPS, 2017
([arXiv:1706.03762](https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.03762)), Table 3.

members.name: transformer_large_seq2seq
members.summary: Construct a Transformer "big" with a tied seq2seq-LM head.
members.extended:
Same trunk as `transformer_large` (N=6, d_model=1024, h=16),
augmented with a linear LM head whose weight is tied to the target-side
embedding when `config.tie_word_embeddings` is `True`.  Use for
high-accuracy translation / summarisation fine-tunes when compute
permits.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description: Reserved for future weight registration; currently a no-op.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Optional `TransformerConfig` field overrides forwarded into
the underlying config.

returns.description: Encoder-decoder trunk wrapped with the tied LM head.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.text.transformer import transformer_large_seq2seq
>>> model = transformer_large_seq2seq().eval()
>>> src = lucid.tensor([[1, 234, 567, 2]])
>>> tgt = lucid.tensor([[1, 100, 200]])
>>> out = model(src, decoder_input_ids=tgt)
>>> out.logits.shape   # (B=1, T_tgt=3, vocab=37000)
(1, 3, 37000)
notes: Reference: Vaswani et al., *"Attention Is All You Need"*, NeurIPS, 2017.

summary: Original Transformer family — Vaswani et al., 2017 (encoder-decoder seq2seq).
citation: Vaswani, Ashish, et al. "Attention Is All You Need." Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, 2017, pp. 5998–6008.
theory:
The Transformer replaces the recurrence of RNN-based seq2seq models
with **scaled dot-product attention**, enabling fully parallel
sequence processing.  For query/key/value matrices
$Q \in \mathbb{R}^{T_q \times d_k}$,
$K \in \mathbb{R}^{T_k \times d_k}$, and
$V \in \mathbb{R}^{T_k \times d_v}$, attention is



$$
\operatorname{Attention}(Q, K, V)
    = \operatorname{softmax}\!\left(
        \frac{Q K^\top}{\sqrt{d_k}}
      \right) V,
$$

where the $1/\sqrt{d_k}$ factor counteracts the variance of
dot products in high dimensions and keeps softmax gradients
well-conditioned.

**Multi-head attention** runs $h$ parallel attention heads on
learned projections of $(Q, K, V)$ into $d_k = d_v = d_{\text{model}} / h$ subspaces, concatenates the outputs, and
applies a final linear:



$$
\operatorname{MultiHead}(Q, K, V) =
    \operatorname{Concat}(\text{head}_1, \dots, \text{head}_h)\, W^O,
\qquad
\text{head}_i = \operatorname{Attention}(Q W_i^Q, K W_i^K, V W_i^V).
$$

Each encoder layer stacks multi-head self-attention and a
position-wise feed-forward network (with hidden width
$d_{\text{ff}}=2048$), wrapped in residual connections and
post-LayerNorm.  Each decoder layer adds **causal self-attention**
over previously generated targets and **cross-attention** over the
encoder output.

Position information is injected via **sinusoidal positional
encodings** of fixed (untrained) frequencies, which generalise to
sequence lengths unseen during training.  The paper's *base* model
($N=6$ encoder/decoder layers, $d_{\text{model}}=512$,
$h=8$) achieves state-of-the-art WMT 2014 En-De / En-Fr BLEU
while training in a fraction of the time of prior recurrent and
convolutional seq2seq systems — the architectural foundation for
essentially every modern language model.
